VAL-CONST-001.detail: ROUTING_TOKENS[Network.Mainnet] contains 4 tokens. WETH (0xC02aaA39b223FE8D0A0e5C4F27eAD9083C756Cc2), USDC (0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48), and USDT (0xdAC17F958D2ee523a2206206994597C13D831ec7) are all present as checksummed addresses. Verified via direct Python import and pytest test_routing_tokens_shape PASSED.

VAL-CONST-002.detail: ROUTING_TOKENS defined for Arbitrum (42161, 3 tokens), Optimism (10, 3 tokens), and Base (8453, 3 tokens). Each chain includes WETH, USDC, and a third routing token (USDT or equivalent), all as valid checksummed addresses. Verified via direct Python import printing all entries and pytest test_routing_tokens_shape PASSED which validates shape, count, and checksum for all L2 chains.

VAL-CONST-003.detail: DAI (0x6B175474E89094C44Da98b954EedeAC495271d0F) is present in ROUTING_TOKENS[Network.Mainnet] as the 4th entry, enabling historical routing for blocks before USDC had deep liquidity. Verified via direct Python import confirming DAI address membership in mainnet routing tokens list.

frictions: BROWNIE_NETWORK_ID env var needed for direct Python imports (y/__init__.py) while BROWNIE_NETWORK env var needed for pytest conftest.py — both must be set for pytest runs.
pytest teardown produces INTERNALERROR (UnicodeDecodeError from brownie scanning .so files) — does not affect test results but makes output noisy. Known pre-existing issue.
Direct Python script hung on process teardown after printing all output (semaphore/process pool cleanup) — required timeout but all output was captured before hang.
